### 🎯 Coverage Score: **100%** (5/5 signal types ✅)

**Bottom line:** The Americas PVC market is in a compound supply-side crisis. Westlake has structurally rationalized North American capacity. Braskem — Brazil's only domestic PVC producer — is financially distressed. Vynova Europe is insolvent. LG Bohai is on force majeure. US producers are the most resilient globally (ethane feedstock, low electricity costs), but tighter supply offsets the cost advantage. Brazil faces the worst exposure: 14%+ tariff on US imports + domestic supplier in financial distress + BRL headwinds. Colombia is fully import-dependent with FTA access but exposed to freight and supply tightness. Mexico and Canada are best-positioned via USMCA and proximity.
